title: Python wrapper for DPI library
body:
It would by nice to be able to use the generated libraries also in a python environment.

Therefore, I wrote a simple small wrapper around the DPI library. The handwritten version works nice but needs updates if I modify the model input or output parameter. An automatic generation would be nice.

I see there different options:
a) verilator generates a systemVerilog file for the DPI lib. A small python script could convert this file in a python package as an add-on if the user requests it. Might be a little bit trick as it needs to parse the sv file. Small modifications in verilator might break the script. 
b) verilator generates a python wrapper like the systemVerilog wrapper for the DPI lib. This would need an additional function in V3ProtectLib.cpp to do the generation but could use the same infrastructure as the SV wrapper.
Draw back: There come next week someone who what's a next language. This makes verilator more complex and needs also maintenance.

If this feature is accepted I can do the work. 
Do you what this feature?
How (option a, b or another way) should it be implemented?
